channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hyundai, Kia recall over 200K electric cars over power issues
item.description: Hyundai’s recall encompasses over 145,000 cars, including some IONIQ 5 and IONIQ 6 EVs along with Genesis GV60, Genesis GV70 and Genesis G80 models.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A look at how some of Trump's picks to lead health agencies could help carry out Kennedy's overhaul
item.description: The team that President-elect Donald Trump has selected to lead federal health agencies in his second administration includes a retired congressman, a surgeon and a former talk-show host.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Texas education board approves optional Bible-infused curriculum for elementary schools
item.description: Texas’ education board voted Friday to allow Bible-infused teachings in elementary schools under optional new curriculum that could test boundaries between religion and public classrooms in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Fox 5

item.title: Blue state to shutter over a dozen migrant shelters as Trump’s set to implement deportation agenda
item.description: New York will close 12 migrant shelters before the end of the year and weeks before President-elect Donald Trump takes office. The crisis is set to cost New York City around $12 billion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voters rejected historic election reforms across the US, despite more than $100M push
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — Two weeks before Election Day, activists from across the country gathered for an online rally heralding the historic number of state ballot initiatives seeking to change the way people vote.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Storm inundates Northern California with rain, heavy snow. Thousands remain in the dark in Seattle
item.description: HEALDSBURG, Calif. (AP) — Heavy downpours fell over much of Northern California on Friday, causing small landslides, overflowing a river and flooding some streets, including in parts of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: NOAH BERGER and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Louisiana lawmakers pass income and corporate tax cuts, raising statewide sales tax to pay for it
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana’s GOP-dominated legislature passed tax cuts on personal and corporate income on Friday in exchange for a statewide sales tax increase, giving Gov.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News contributor Janette Nesheiwat tapped as Trump's surgeon general
item.description: President-elect Trump named Janette Nesheiwat, a Fox News contributor and medical doctor in New York and New Jersey, as his nominee for surgeon general in his second administration. "I am proud to announce that Dr. Janette Nesheiwat will be the Nation’s Doctor as the United States Surgeon General.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: John Hopkins surgeon and COVID contrarian Marty Makary selected to lead Trump’s FDA
item.description: President-elect Trump has picked Marty Makary, a Johns Hopkins's surgeon who has espoused contrarian views about the COVID-19 pandemic, to lead the Federal Drug Administration. Makary is a surgical oncologist at Johns Hopkins University and an author.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump expected to name investor Scott Bessent as Treasury secretary pick
item.description: Bessent, founder and CEO of hedge fund Key Square Group, served as an economic adviser to the Trump campaign, often appearing on financial talk shows to argue in favor of the president-elect's economic policies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Amazon purchase leaves Las Vegas senior couple in refund limbo, company responds
item.description: “If this happened to me, it's had to have happened to other people,” says Las Vegas senior LaRae Obenauf.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Driver in crash that killed 4 kids was allegedly photographing ‘Welcome to Florida’ sign
item.description: A New York man was extradited to Florida this month after he was accused of causing a deadly crash when he came to a near-stop on Interstate 95 to take photo of the "Welcome to Florida" sign.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 convicted in human smuggling case after Indian family froze to death on US-Canada border
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — A jury convicted two men on Friday of charges related to human smuggling for their roles in an international operation that led to the deaths of a family of Indian migrants who froze while trying to cross the Canada-U.S. border during a 2022 blizzard.
item.dc:creator: MARK VANCLEAVE and MICHAEL GOLDBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colorado funeral home owners plead guilty to corpse abuse after nearly 200 bodies found decomposing
item.description: Two funeral home owners in Colorado pleaded guilty to 191 counts of abuse of a corpse on Friday, a year after decaying bodies were found piled on top of each other.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las Vegas man killed by police while on 911 call
item.description: Brandon Durham, 43, of Las Vegas, died at the home on Tuesday, Nov. 12. Police said he was pronounced deceased at a hospital a short time thereafter.
item.dc:creator: Kyle J. Paine, NewsNation

item.title: Cases of 'walking pneumonia' are rising across the US
item.description: Infections resulting in "walking pneumonia" are generally less severe than pneumonia, with many patients going through their daily routines.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Rousseau, NewsNation

item.title: McDonald's unveils new value menu, extends $5 Meal Deal
item.description: McDonald’s is offering its customers more ways to save on Big Macs, McNuggets, and other food items beginning in the New Year. The fast-food giant announced on Friday that it will launch a new everyday value menu on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Youngkin announces order to protect children from social media
item.description: Governor Glenn Youngkin issued an executive order on Thursday in the hopes of protecting children from addictive social media platforms.
item.dc:creator: Madie MacDonald, NewsNation

item.title: Utah city among the 10 'least sinful' cities in the United States
item.description: Denver was ranked as the No. 6 most "sinful" city in the U.S., ahead of cities like New York City and Miami in a new ranking released by WalletHub.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Brazilian illegal immigrant wanted for drug trafficking arrested in Massachusetts sanctuary city
item.description: Federal immigration authorities appear to have stepped up arrests of illegal immigrant criminal suspects in Massachusetts in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about a Wisconsin man who faked his own death and fled to Eastern Europe
item.description: GREEN LAKE, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning this summer and left his wife and three children has been located in Eastern Europe and is communicating with law enforcement, but he has not committed to returning home, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman in coma after trying to save pro golfer's 6-year-old son from drowning
item.description: Professional golfer Brian Harman revealed that a close family friend has been in a coma for weeks after trying to save his 6-year-old son from a near-drowning.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, Fox 5

item.title: Democrats rattled by postelection changes
item.description: The Democratic Party is grappling with the aftermath of its blowout loss in the presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas locals will not be shocked to learn which city ranked 'Most Sinful' in 2024
item.description: Denver was ranked as the No. 6 most "sinful" city in the U.S., ahead of cities like New York City and Miami in a new ranking released by WalletHub.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florist gets death threats after refusing to serve Trump voters
item.description: The owner posted on social media that the business is now temporarily closed after receiving several death threats. She also made all of her social media accounts private.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Knox, Fox 5

item.title: Fugitive arrested after Chicago standoff blocks away from DNC is being returned to Mississippi
item.description: At the time of the escape, the fugitive was set to make a court appearance on Mississippi charges of attempted murder and armed robbery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump names ambassador to NATO
item.description: President-elect Trump has named his former acting attorney general, Matthew Whitaker, as the next ambassador to NATO, selecting him to fill the role at a crucial time amid Russia's war in Ukraine and tensions over defense spending across the Western security alliance.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, ABC 4

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy lay out plans for 'mass' federal layoffs, rule rollbacks under Trump
item.description: Tech entrepreneurs Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy said Wednesday that their brand-new government efficiency panel will identify “thousands” of regulations for President-elect Donald Trump to eliminate, which they argue will justify “mass head-count reductions” across government.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ukraine has seen success in building clean energy, which is harder for Russia to destroy
item.description: BAKU, Azerbaijan (AP) — Maxim Timchenko, who runs the largest private energy company in Ukraine, pulls out a piece of paper with bar charts showing how much new electricity his company has brought online this year in the country versus how much Russian bombs have destroyed.
item.dc:creator: PETER PRENGAMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Operation Santa: How to adopt a child's letter to the North Pole
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service's Operation Santa program is underway, allowing generous postal service customers to adopt letters from less fortunate children and families, and send them gifts in time for the holidays.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WGN 9

item.title: The worst passwords you could have in 2024 — or any year
item.description: Don't rely on easy-to-remember passwords too much — because all of us tend to think more alike than we'd think.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WGN 9
